user_message:
You are running as part of compound-engineering:ce-doc-review. Review the document `docs/plans/2026-06-20-002-docs-comprehensive-project-manual-plan.md`.

Document type: plan
Origin: none
Decision primer: <prior-decisions>Round 1 — no prior decisions.</prior-decisions>

Return ONLY valid JSON with keys: reviewer, findings, residual_risks, deferred_questions. Findings must use severity P0/P1/P2/P3, finding_type error/omission, autofix_class safe_auto/gated_auto/manual, confidence 50/75/100 only, and evidence as an array of direct quotes from the document. Suppress style-only or speculative findings. Focus only on premise/strategic consequences, adoption/cognitive-load impact, opportunity cost, and goal-work alignment.
assistant_summary: {"reviewer":"product-lens","findings":[{"title":"Manual premise lacks pain evidence","severity":"P1","section":"Problem Frame","why_it_matters":"The plan may optimize for comprehensiveness before proving that a continuous A-to-Z manual is the highest-leverage fix. If the real pain is navigation, onboarding, or a few drift-prone references, implementers could spend a large docs effort creating another surface instead of a smaller docs map or targeted guide set.","finding_type":"omission","autofix_class":"manual","confidence":75,"evidence":["The repository now has enough product surface that a new user, agent consumer, or maintainer must jump across many files to understand the whole system.","The current docs are stronger after the README/spec refresh, but they are optimized as entry points and references, not as one continuous A-to-Z explanation.","The requested artifact is intentionally broad: it should help a reader build a mental model first, then drill into operational details without treating the codebase as an unexplained feature inventory."],"suggested_fix":"Add a short premise section that cites concrete evidence for the manual-sized problem and explicitly rejects the lower-cost alternative of a navigation hub plus targeted gap fixes."},{"title":"Audience paths are underspecified","severity":"P2","section":"Target Deliverable / Audiences","why_it_matters":"A single comprehensive manual can reduce cross-file hunting while still overwhelming the exact adoption audiences it names. Without required reader paths, new users and agent consumers may be forced through maintainer and reviewer material before finding their next action.","finding_type":"omission","autofix_class":"gated_auto","confidence":75,"evidence":["Shape: a single long-form manual with chapter-level source links and an appendix that maps each major claim to repository evidence.","R4. The manual serves users, agent consumers, contributors, maintainers, and reviewers without requiring them to read every existing doc first.","New user: wants to install, enable, run, and understand what problem EntireContext solves.","Maintainer: wants release discipline, ADR/decision reuse policy, contract-drift guards, troubleshooting, and operational side effects."],"suggested_fix":"Require chapter 1 to include an audience-path table that tells each named audience which chapters to read first, which to skip initially, and what question they should be able to answer afterward."}],"residual_risks":["The plan mitigates drift with source links and an evidence appendix, but the long-term update burden still depends on future documentation ownership discipline."],"deferred_questions":["Before execution, decide whether the manual is meant to be a primary onboarding artifact or a maintainer/reference artifact with README remaining the actual onboarding path."]}
